---
title: "Deploy walkthrough"
subtitle: "Testnet → mainnet, the right way."
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Status:** Stub. Full chapter coming soon.
:::

## The question

Your program compiles, your tests pass, you've run it locally. Now you want
to deploy. What does the actual deploy command look like? What can go wrong?
When are you ready for mainnet?

## What this chapter will cover

### Testnet deployment

```bash
leo deploy \
  --network testnet \
  --endpoint https://api.explorer.provable.com/v1 \
  --priority-fees 100000 \
  --broadcast
```

- Why `--broadcast` is required (and what happens silently if you forget)
- Why `--priority-fees` is plural (and what happens if you set it to 0)
- How to verify deployment on aleoscan in the next ~60 seconds
- What "deployed but not visible" usually means (priority fee too low; resubmit)

### Mainnet readiness checklist

- ✅ Tests pass with `leo test --prove` (full proof generation, not just interpreter)
- ✅ Cross-program tests pass with leo-bindings against `LocalVM`
- ✅ Privacy review (see [Merkle trees](../02-cryptographic-primitives/02-merkle-trees.qmd) and the
  privacy patterns chapters) — no public mappings keyed by user identity, etc.
- ✅ Constructor decorator is correct: `@noupgrade` for immutable, `@admin(...)`
  for governed, `@checksum(...)` for DAO-controlled
- ✅ All imports resolve to mainnet program IDs (no `test_` prefix)
- ✅ Audit (or at least a peer review) of the privacy invariants
- ✅ DPS or external signing for proof generation if you have non-developer users

### Mainnet deployment

Same command, with `--network mainnet` and a higher priority fee. **Confirm
twice before broadcasting** — there is no `leo undeploy`.

## Interactive viz planned

A "deployment dashboard" simulator:
- Paste a `program.json` and a `main.leo`
- Watch the simulator predict gas, fee, and broadcast outcome
- See common error modes (program already exists, insufficient balance, fee too low) before you hit them on the real network

## Companion

This chapter's command structure is also encoded in the
[aleo-skills](https://github.com/casualcomputer/aleo-skills) Claude Code
plugin. If you `/plugin install casualcomputer/aleo-skills`, the always-on
rules will warn you about `--broadcast` / `--priority-fees` automatically
when you ask Claude to deploy.
